document.title: IEC 62133-2 Test Report for Rechargeable Li-ion Battery MBP-3S1P3330-HAZ / 3ICP4/60/80
document.revisionOrVersion: Report No. C24B017-M0A0; TRF No. IEC62133_2C; Master TRF Dated 2022-07-01

sourceSummary.oneLine: CB Scheme IEC 62133-2 test report for mPLUS Electronics rechargeable Li-ion battery model MBP-3S1P3330-HAZ / 3ICP4/60/80.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports assessment of tested rechargeable Li-ion battery safety compliance under IEC 62133-2, Korean national differences, and listed EN 62133-2 requirements; the report states it is not valid as a CB Test Report unless signed by an approved IECEE Testing Laboratory and appended to a CB Test Certificate issued by an NCB.
keyPoints: Report number C24B017-M0A0 was issued on 2024-09-30 by CTK Co., Ltd.
Applicant, manufacturer, and factory are listed as mPLUS Electronics Co., Ltd. in Icheon-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
Test item is a Rechargeable Li-ion Battery, model MBP-3S1P3330-HAZ / 3ICP4/60/80, rated 11.55 Vdc, 3330 mAh, 38.46 Wh.
The product fulfils the requirements of KC 62133-2(2020-07), EN 62133-2:2017, and EN 62133-2:2017/A1:2021.
Battery tests reported pass verdicts for case stress, external short-circuit, free fall, over-charging, vibration, and mechanical shock.

evidence.claim: The source is a test report issued under IEC 62133-2.
evidence.evidenceText: TEST REPORT IEC 62133-2 Secondary cells and batteries containing alkaline or other non-acid electrolytes – Safety requirements for portable sealed secondary cells, and for batteries made from them, for use in portable applications – Part 2: Lithium systems

evidence.claim: The report date of issue is 2024-09-30.
CTK Co., Ltd. prepared the report and is listed as the CB Testing Laboratory.
evidence.evidenceText: Name of Testing Laboratory preparing the Report : CTK Co., Ltd.; CB Testing Laboratory: CTK Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The applicant is mPLUS Electronics Co., Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: Applicant’s name : mPLUS Electronics Co., Ltd

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is mPLUS Electronics Co., Ltd. in the Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer : mPLUS Electronics Co., Ltd 152-10, Muchon-ro, Bubal-eup, Icheon-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: The applicable test specification standards are IEC 62133-2:2017 and IEC 62133-2:2017/AMD1:2021.
The test procedure is CB Scheme.
The test item is a Rechargeable Li-ion Battery with model/type reference MBP-3S1P3330-HAZ / 3ICP4/60/80.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item description : Rechargeable Li-ion Battery; Model/Type reference : MBP-3S1P3330-HAZ / 3ICP4/60/80

evidence.claim: The battery rating is 11.55 Vdc, 3330 mAh, 38.46 Wh.
The battery cell model is 396080PU and the battery cell configuration is 3S1P.

evidence.evidenceText: The product fulfils the requirements of KC 62133-2(2020-07), EN 62133-2:2017, EN 62133-2:2017/A1:2021

evidence.claim: Republic of Korea national differences are included as Attachment 1.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 1: 3 pages (Republic of Korea) NATIONAL DIFFERENCES (National standard KC62133-2(2020-07)

evidence.claim: Battery tests performed included charging procedure, case stress, external short-circuit, free fall, over-charging, vibration, and mechanical shock.
evidence.evidenceText: Tests performed: cl.7.1 Charging procedure for test purposes; cl.7.2.2 Case stress at high ambient temperature (battery); cl.7.3.2 External short-circuit (battery); cl.7.3.3 Free fall (battery); cl.7.3.6 Over-charging of battery (battery); cl.7.3.8 Mechanical tests (battery); -7.3.8.1 Vibration (battery); -7.3.8.2 Mechanical shock (battery)

evidence.claim: The tested battery passed external short-circuit testing with no fire or explosion.
evidence.evidenceText: 7.3.2 External short-circuit (battery) P; Results: no fire, no explosion : (See appended table 7.3.2) P

evidence.claim: The tested battery passed over-charging testing with no fire or explosion.
evidence.evidenceText: 7.3.6 Over-charging of battery P; Results: no fire, no explosion : (See appended table 7.3.6) P

evidence.claim: The tested battery passed vibration and mechanical shock tests with no fire, explosion, rupture, leakage, or venting.
evidence.evidenceText: 7.3.8.1 Vibration P Results: no fire, no explosion, no rupture, no leakage or venting; 7.3.8.2 Mechanical shock P Results: no leakage, no venting, no rupture, no explosion and no fire

evidence.claim: The report states it is not valid as a CB Test Report unless signed by an approved IECEE Testing Laboratory and appended to a CB Test Certificate issued by an NCB.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is not valid as a CB Test Report unless signed by an approved IECEE Testing Laboratory and appended to a CB Test Certificate issued by an NCB in accordance with IECEE 02.

reviewFlags.reason: The Issuing NCB name is not present in the extracted source text, although the report states authenticity can be verified by contacting the responsible NCB.

The source path suggests an approval certificate folder, but the provided text is a test report and states it is not valid as a CB Test Report unless signed and appended to a CB Test Certificate issued by an NCB.
No explicit CB Test Certificate number is present in the provided source text.
